title: odontites vulgaris subsp. salinus
intro: Odontites vulgaris subsp. salinus, also known as Salt tooth-knapweed or Salt odontites, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include the potential treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, infused oil, or dried herb for various herbal preparations.
